description: Bind an npm scope to a private registry and configure its auth, at user level on the Renovate runner. Self-contained: does NOT require `base-private-reg-setup`. Can be extended multiple times, once per scope — each call targets its own registry and its own token env var, so you can chain scopes pointing to different registries. Merges into the postUpgradeTasks of Otter's built-in task presets (tasks/base, tasks/otter-ng-update, tasks/sdk-regenerate). MUST be extended BEFORE those task presets. arg0: npm scope name without the leading '@' (e.g. 'myorg'). arg1: scheme-less registry URL with trailing slash (e.g. '//repository.rnd.amadeus.net/api/npm/npm/'); used to derive the registry URL ('https:{{arg1}}') and the auth entry key ('{{arg1}}:_auth'). arg2: name of the environment variable holding the base64-encoded token (e.g. 'NPM_AUTH_BASE64_MYORG'); must be exposed to Renovate via customEnvVariables / allowedEnv. Writes to ~/.npmrc on the runner so nothing is committed.
